title: 10 best herbal creams for post nasal drip

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal purple coneflower creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like alkamides and lignans, which help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and postnasal drip.

Antimicrobial properties: The cream's antimicrobial properties, thanks to the presence of compounds like rosin and sesquiterpenes, can help combat bacterial and viral infections that contribute to postnasal drip, promoting healing and reducing symptoms.

Soften mucus and promote expectoration: Purple coneflower creams contain mucilages and saponins that help soften thick mucus, making it easier to expel and reduce postnasal drip, while also soothing and protecting the delicate nasal passages.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with post nasal drip because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and polysaccharides, help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. By soothing and calming the mucous membranes, Echinacea purpurea cream can alleviate the discomfort and irritation caused by post nasal drip, promoting a sense of relief and comfort for those affected. Its natural and soothing properties make it a popular alternative remedy.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds, particularly echinacoside and isoechinacoside, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating post nasal drip.

Flavonoids (specifically, Quercetin and Kaempferol): As potent antioxidants, these flavonoids have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties, which can help break down and clear excess mucus from the nasal passages, reducing post nasal drip.

Polysaccharides (specifically, Arabinogalactan): This complex carbohydrate has been found to have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: Alkaloids, specifically echinacoside and cichoric acid, are found in the roots, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
Leaves: Echinacoside and other compounds found in the leaves may help reduce inflammation and ease congestion associated with post nasal drip.
Flowers: Cichoric acid and other compounds in the flowers have anti-inflammatory properties and may help soothe and calm the nasal passages and sinuses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers at dawn when dew is still present.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at 100°f for 2 hours or until brittle.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried flowers with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 5 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a glass jar and let it cool for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a key compound found in licorice root, helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated mucous membranes, making it easier for the body to clear out excess mucus and alleviate postnasal drip symptoms.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice creams have antioxidant properties that help to combat oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and congestion associated with postnasal drip.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of glycoside found in licorice root, exhibit anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to thin out mucus and ease its passage through the nasal passages, making it easier to expel and reducing the sensation of postnasal drip.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with post nasal drip because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and throat, thereby alleviating post nasal drip. Additionally, licorice creams have a mucolytic effect, helping to break down mucus and promote its clearance from the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion and discomfort associated with post nasal drip. This can lead to improved breathing and overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin (Glycyrrhizin): It acts as a demulcent, coating the mucous membranes and providing relief from irritation and inflammation associated with post nasal drip.

Flavonoids (Isolaricinolide): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and infection in the nasal passages, alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.

Saponins (Glycyrrhizinic acid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing the sensation of post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a key compound responsible for the anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties needed to alleviate post-nasal drip.

Leaves: Although less commonly used than roots and bark, the leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin and are sometimes used to create milder creams that still provide relief from post-nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500g of dried root of glycyrrhiza glabra and 200ml of sweet almond oil.
Infuse the root in the sweet almond oil at a temperature of 60-70°c for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container to remove solids.
Add 100g of beeswax to the oil and heat at 70-80°c until melted and combined.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 2-3 hours before applying to skin.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause postnasal drip, reducing inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.

Decongestant properties: Thyme contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have natural decongestant properties that help to reduce swelling in the nasal tissues, relieving sinus pressure and clearing mucus from the airways.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing postnasal drip.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and alleviating the sensation of excess mucus. Additionally, thyme's expectorant properties help to thin and clear mucus, providing relief from post nasal drip. This makes thyme creams a popular natural remedy for sinus issues.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol, a phenolic compound, helps to reduce the viscosity of mucus, making it easier to expel and alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol, a monoterpenic phenol, exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help to eliminate the underlying bacterial or fungal infections that may be contributing to post nasal drip.

Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide, a sesquiterpenoid, has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of thymol, a natural antiseptic and decongestant.
Stems: The stems of Thymus vulgaris contain thymol and other essential oils that help to reduce nasal congestion and promote drainage.
Buds: Thyme buds, rich in thymol and other bioactive compounds, are used to create creams that help to clear nasal passages and reduce post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 to 20 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers on a dry sunny morning.
Steam distill the plant material to obtain 2 to 3 milliliters of essential oil in 30 minutes.
Combine 50 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of jojoba oil in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Mix 2 to 3 milliliters of the thymus vulgaris essential oil with 10 milliliters of distilled water.
Stir the mixture until it thickens and reaches a smooth consistency in 10 to 15 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Lemon balm cream's antihistamine properties help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating post-nasal drip symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemon balm cream, such as rosmarinic acid and citral, help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with post-nasal drip.
Expectorant properties: Lemon balm's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear excess mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing the sensation of post-nasal drip.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with post nasal drip because of its natural antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Melissa officinalis, such as rosmarinic acid and tannins, have been shown to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages. By soothing and calming the nasal mucosa, Melissa officinalis creams can help alleviate the symptoms of post nasal drip, providing quick relief and promoting a healthy respiratory system. This natural remedy can be a welcome alternative to traditional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, helping to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
Borneol: A monoterpene with expectorant properties, which helps to thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from post nasal drip.
Geranial: A citrus-based monoterpene with antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi in the nasal passages that contribute to post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melissa officinalis are commonly used due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.

Flowers: The flowers of Melissa officinalis are also widely used in creams for post nasal drip due to their high content of essential oils and flavonoids, which exhibit decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.

Buds: The buds of Melissa officinalis are sometimes used in creams for post nasal drip because they contain a high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids, which can help to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the melissa flowers in the morning after the dew has dried but before the heat of the day sets in.
Steam distill the fresh melissa flowers using 10 kg of flowers and 2 kg of water for 2 hours.
Combine 10 grams of beeswax with 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt.
Add 5 ml of melissa essential oil to the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture and stir.
Pour the melted mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian Blue Gum contains compounds that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, which can ease congestion and discomfort caused by post-nasal drip.

Decongestant: The eucalyptol content in Tasmanian Blue Gum cream acts as a natural decongestant, helping to thin and clear excess mucus from the nasal passages, alleviating stuffiness and pressure associated with post-nasal drip.

Antimicrobial: Tasmanian Blue Gum's antimicrobial properties can help combat infections that may contribute to post-nasal drip, such as sinusitis or tonsillitis, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with post nasal drip because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol and eucalyptol present in these creams work by thinning mucus, making it easier to expel from the nasal passages. This helps to alleviate congestion and reduce the sensation of post nasal drip, providing relief from the discomfort and irritation associated with excessive mucus production in the nasal passages. It also helps to soothe and calm the nasal tissues.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): As a natural expectorant and mucolytic agent, cineole helps thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to drain and relieving congestion associated with post nasal drip.

Furanocoumarins (especially piperitone and limonene): These compounds exhibit antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the growth of microorganisms that can contribute to post nasal drip and alleviate inflammation in the nasal passages.

Flavonoids (especially rutin and quercetin): These polyphenolic compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating symptoms of post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high eucalyptol content, which acts as a natural expectorant to relieve congestion.
Barks: The barks are used because they contain salicylic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and provides relief from post-nasal drip symptoms.
Buds: Eucalyptus buds are used due to their high eucalyptol and camphor content, which helps to break down mucus and provide quick relief from congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from a clean and safe environment.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 cup of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 2 cups of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Infuse the mixture in a double boiler for 4 hours then strain through a cheesecloth.
Mix the infused oil with 1 cup of shea butter and 1/2 cup of beeswax in a saucepan.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elderberry's antioxidant compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, alleviating symptoms of postnasal drip.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and anthocyanins present in elderberry have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal tissues, making it easier to breathe and reducing postnasal drip.

Mucolytic properties: Elderberry's mucolytic compounds break down thick mucus secretions, loosening and clearing them from the nasal passages, which helps to relieve congestion and postnasal drip symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as elder, creams helps with post nasal drip because of its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages. As a natural expectorant, Sambucus nigra cream helps to thin and clear mucus, providing relief from the discomfort and irritation associated with post nasal drip. This natural remedy can be especially beneficial for those who experience chronic or seasonal congestion.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating the symptoms of post nasal drip.
Flavonoids (Quercetin): Quercetin, a type of flavonoid, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect the nasal mucosa, reducing post nasal drip.
Sambunigrin: This glycoside compound has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that contribute to post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Sambucus nigra for making creams to treat post-nasal drip due to their high content of bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and polyphenols, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: The leaves are another widely used part, as they contain similar bioactive compounds to the flowers, which help to reduce nasal congestion and soothe the respiratory tract.
Fruits: The fruits of Sambucus nigra are also used, as they contain anthocyanins and other antioxidants that can help to alleviate symptoms of post-nasal drip, such as inflammation and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves on a sunny day with minimal rain.
Combine the harvested sambucus nigra flowers and leaves with 200 ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container, discard the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of emulsifying wax in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages and throat, alleviating congestion and discomfort associated with postnasal drip.

Antihistamine and antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's histamine-blocking effects and muscle-relaxing properties can help calm down allergic reactions and spasms in the nasal tissues, reducing the frequency and severity of postnasal drip episodes.

Soothing and calming effects: The flavonoids and terpenoids present in chamomile have a sedative and calming effect on the mucous membranes, promoting relaxation and reducing the urge to cough or sneeze excessively, which can further exacerbate postnasal drip.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with post nasal drip because it contains soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that calm the nasal passages and sinuses. The cream's cooling sensation helps to reduce congestion and itching, providing relief from the discomfort associated with post nasal drip. Additionally, chamomile's natural anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling in the nasal tissues, allowing for improved drainage and a decrease in the amount of mucus produced. This leads to a reduction in post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: Apigenin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic properties, which can help reduce nasal congestion and soothe the nasal passages to alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.

Bisabolol: Bisabolol, a sesquiterpene alcohol, has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and soothing properties, which can help calm the nasal mucosa, reduce inflammation, and prevent bacterial and viral infections that may contribute to post nasal drip.

Matricine: Matricine, a sesquiterpene lactone, has anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, which can help reduce inflammation, relax the muscles in the nasal passages, and promote the expulsion of mucus to alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm the nasal passages and reduce congestion.
Leaves: Leaves contain apigenin, an antioxidant that has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds are used to create an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic oil, which can be incorporated into creams to help soothe and protect the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a reputable supplier.
Combine 1/2 cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 2 cups of distilled water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes then let it cool for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl discarding the solids.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of the cooled liquid.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula (pot marigold) cream helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, alleviating congestion and discomfort associated with post-nasal drip.
Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold's antimicrobial compounds inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can contribute to post-nasal drip, promoting a healthy nasal environment.

Soothing and protective properties: Calendula cream forms a physical barrier on the skin, providing a soothing and protecting effect on irritated nasal passages, helping to reduce discomfort and promote healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with post nasal drip because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing swelling and congestion. This helps to block the excessive mucus flow associated with post nasal drip. Additionally, Calendula's antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that may contribute to nasal drip. By applying the cream to the affected area, individuals may experience relief from post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic Acids: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, alleviating the discomfort associated with post nasal drip.

Triterpenoids (specifically, Calendula oleanolic acid): These compounds have shown antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the nasal mucosa, thereby alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.

N-alkanoyl-4-hydroxyanthranilates (specifically, Calendulin): These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and protect the nasal mucosa, reducing the likelihood of post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high content of anti-inflammatory compounds and their ability to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes.
Leaves: They are also used to make creams due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat infection and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers in full sun at peak bloom when petals are fully opened and yellow.
Dry calendula flowers using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 3-4 hours.
Make a calendula infusion by steeping 1 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of hot water for 10 minutes.
Combine 1/2 cup of infused calendula liquid with 1/4 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture has cooled to 120 degrees fahrenheit.

remedy_properties: Antihistamine and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Rosemary contains compounds like camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate, which have antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate postnasal drip.

Expectorant and Decongestant Properties: Rosemary's essential oils can help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, reducing the severity of postnasal drip by promoting drainage and relieving congestion.

Antioxidant and Antimicrobial Properties: Rosemary's antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, may help combat underlying infections or inflammation in the respiratory tract that can contribute to postnasal drip.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, creams helps with post nasal drip because of its natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in rosemary creams, such as camphor and eucalyptus, help to thin out mucus and reduce congestion in the nasal passages. By applying rosemary creams to the chest and nose, users can experience relief from post nasal drip, allowing them to breathe more easily and feel more comfortable. This natural remedy provides a soothing and effective solution for those struggling with post nasal drip.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic diterpene has potent antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nasal mucosa, leading to a decrease in post nasal drip.

Bornyl acetate: This terpene has decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to clear mucus from the nasal passages and reduce swelling, thereby alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high content of rosmarinic acid, a compound that has decongestant properties.
Flowers: Flowers are used because they contain a high amount of essential oils, including rosmarinic acid and other compounds that can help alleviate nasal congestion.
Stems: Stems are used as they contain a significant amount of rosmarinic acid and other bioactive compounds that can help relieve post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 2 tablespoons of beeswax to create herbal cream base.
Mix 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/2 cup of shea butter in a double boiler at 100°f to 120°f.
Add 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil and 2 teaspoons of rosemary essential oil to the oil mixture.
Combine the oil mixture with the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a blender and blend for 30 seconds.
Stir the mixture and add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the mixture and heat until melted and combined.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, allowing for easier drainage and relief from post-nasal drip.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of plantain cream can help to combat infection-causing bacteria and viruses that may contribute to post-nasal drip, promoting a healthy nasal environment and reducing symptoms.

Mucilant properties: Plantain's mucilant properties create a soothing, protective barrier on the mucous membranes, helping to reduce irritation and inflammation caused by post-nasal drip and allowing for easier movement of mucus through the nasal passages.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantago major, also known as plantain, creams helps with post nasal drip because of its anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The plantain plant has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, and its creams have been found to ease nasal congestion and soothe irritated nasal passages. The active compounds in plantain, such as aucubin and baicalein, help to reduce swelling and promote drainage, providing relief from post nasal drip and associated discomfort. This makes plantain creams a popular natural remedy for alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Aucubin: Aucubin, a phenylethanoid glycoside, has anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties that help break down mucus and reduce its viscosity, making it easier to expel excess mucus from the nasal passages.

Aristolochic acid: Although it's a controversial compound due to its potential carcinogenic effects, aristolochic acid in Plantago major has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate nasal congestion and post nasal drip.

Iridoid glycosides (e.g., aucubin, plantaginin): These compounds have been found to possess mucolytic and expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, thus reducing post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are often used due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and protect the nasal passages.
Seeds: The seeds may be used as they are believed to contain anti-inflammatory properties that could help alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
Roots: The roots might be used due to their reported expectorant properties, which could help thin mucus and make it easier to expel.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried plantago major leaves and flowers and mix with 10g of beeswax in a heat-proof bowl.
Melt the beeswax mixture in a double boiler over low heat for about 20 minutes.
Add 30g of sweet almond oil and 20g of coconut oil to the melted beeswax and stir well.
Stir in 10g of vitamin e oil and 10g of aloe vera gel to enhance skin benefits.
Pour the herbal mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify for about 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry creams helps with post nasal drip because of their unique combination of soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The extract from highbush cranberries contains potent compounds that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing mucus to drain more easily and alleviating congestion. Additionally, the natural emollients in the cream provide long-lasting hydration to the mucous membranes, helping to thin out thickened mucus and promote its clearance from the nasal cavity.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Highbush cranberries contain compounds like anthocyanins, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The berries' flavonoids and phenolic acids work to reduce swelling and alleviate congestion, helping to ease postnasal drip symptoms.
Mucolytic properties: The creams' mucilage content helps break down thick mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing the discomfort associated with postnasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce congestion and soothe irritated mucous membranes.
Barks: Contains tannins and other bioactive compounds that aid in decongesting the nasal passages and reducing excess mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest vaccinium macrocarpon berries in late summer or early fall when they are ripe and dry them thoroughly for up to 24 hours.
Grind 50 grams of dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for about 5 minutes.
Combine the powdered vaccinium macrocarpon with 25 grams of beeswax, 15 grams of coconut oil, and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler over low heat.
Stir the mixture gently until it reaches a temperature of 180°f to 190°f and melts completely, taking about 20 minutes, then remove from heat.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer or spoon for about 2 minutes until it becomes smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated nasal passages. The cream's potent ingredients, such as taraxasterol and chicoric acid, work to reduce swelling and congestion, providing instant relief from the constant dripping sensation in the back of the throat. Additionally, dandelion's antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to post nasal drip, promoting a healthy respiratory system and eliminating excess mucus production.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol, help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and postnasal drip.
Mucus-thinning properties: The cream's saponins, particularly hederagenin, help break down excess mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing postnasal drip symptoms like coughing and sore throats.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion cream's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin A and polyphenols, help protect the nasal mucosa from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic sinusitis and postnasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the nasal passages affected by post nasal drip.
Barks: The barks of the dandelion plant contain flavonoids that have antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion associated with post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers, drying them thoroughly for future use within 24 hours.
Combine 100g dried taraxacum officinale with 200ml sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar, stirring well to combine.
Infuse the mixture by placing it in a double boiler at 60°c for 4 hours, allowing the herbal extract to steep and combine with the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids after 30 minutes of pressure-free draining.
Mix the filtered oil with 10% beeswax by weight, heating it gently in a double boiler at 60°c for 15-20 minutes until fully incorporated and smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with post nasal drip because of their soothing, anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's natural ingredients, such as avenanthramides, help to calm irritated mucous membranes in the nose and sinuses, reducing congestion and excess mucus production. This can provide relief from the uncomfortable sensation of post nasal drip, allowing for easier breathing and swallowing. Additionally, oats creams often contain other herbal extracts, like eucalyptus and peppermint, which further aid in decongesting and opening up airways.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Oat creams contain avenanthramides, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated nasal passages, providing relief from congestion and discomfort.
Soothing and moisturizing properties: Herbal oats are rich in beta-glucans, which help lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated mucous membranes, reducing the sensation of post-nasal drip.

Antioxidant properties: Oat creams contain antioxidants that help protect the nasal passages from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy balance of mucus production and drainage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes.
Buds: Contain a unique combination of flavonoids and phenolic acids that provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits to reduce postnasal drip symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh avena sativa leaves and flowers and dry them properly in shade for at least two weeks.
Grind dried herbs into fine powder using mortar and pestle and measure out 20g of the powdered herb.
Combine ground herb with 50ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and let it steep for four hours under the shade.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the herbal extract from the oil and discard solids.
Mix the extracted oil with beeswax pellets at a ratio of 1:1 and melt the mixture in a double boiler until it reaches 160°f for about 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes. The cream's active ingredients, such as camphor and menthol, help to thin out excess mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion. Additionally, sage's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out trapped secretions, providing relief from the constant drips and discomfort associated with post nasal drip. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for managing this common condition.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage cream's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause post-nasal drip, reducing congestion and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in sage cream, such as camphor and salicylic acid, help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating symptoms of post-nasal drip.

Expectorant properties: Sage cream's expectorant properties, particularly due to its eucalyptol content, help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from congestion and coughing associated with post-nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and reducing nasal congestion caused by post nasal drip.
Leaves: Sage leaves contain antioxidants and essential oils that help to thin mucus and clear out the nasal passages, providing relief from post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves and stems and clean thoroughly with cold water.
Combine the harvested material with 1 cup of olive oil in a clean glass jar and infuse for 4-6 weeks at room temperature.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth into a clean container discarding solids and measuring out 100ml of the liquid extract.
Mix the salvia officinalis extract with 50g of beeswax, 20g of shea butter, and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and let cool at room temperature before sealing tightly and storing in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as anethole and fenchone, help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier drainage of mucus. Additionally, fennel's natural expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out thick, sticky mucus that can cause postnasal drip. By reducing inflammation and promoting drainage, herbal fennel creams provide effective relief from congestion and postnasal drip symptoms.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Fennel's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, reducing congestion and alleviating postnasal drip.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling in the nasal tissues and sinuses, which can contribute to postnasal drip.

Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidant properties help protect the nasal mucosa from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate postnasal drip and other respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and congestion associated with post nasal drip.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers measuring one tablespoon.
Combine the dried herbs with two tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 20-30 minutes stirring occasionally until fragrant.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Allow the infused oil to cool and solidify at room temperature taking approximately two hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as salicylic acid and flavonoids, work to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier drainage and relief from congestion. Additionally, stinging nettle's natural antihistamine effects help to combat excess mucus production, further reducing postnasal drip symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle cream helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, which can alleviate symptoms of postnasal drip such as congestion and discomfort.

Natural decongestant properties: The cream's natural ingredients, including stinging nettle and other herbs, help to constrict blood vessels and reduce mucus production, making it easier to breathe and reducing the sensation of postnasal drip.

Antihistamine properties: Stinging nettle cream contains natural antihistamines that help to block histamine receptors in the body, which can alleviate symptoms of postnasal drip such as itchiness, sneezing, and runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a good source of anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help alleviate congestion and relieve sinus pressure associated with post nasal drip.
Barks: The barks contain bioactive compounds like salicylic acid, which has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for soothing irritated mucous membranes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried nettle leaves from a reliable source in amounts of 2 cups for this recipe.
Grind the dried nettle leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 3 minutes.
Combine the nettle powder with 1/4 cup of beeswax pellets, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring frequently, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches a temperature of 180°f within 10 minutes.
Remove from heat, let cool slightly, then whip with a spatula until creamy and smooth before pouring into glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with post nasal drip because they contain allantoin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and calms irritated mucous membranes in the nose and throat. The cream's rich, emollient properties also help to moisturize and protect the skin around the nostrils and mouth, reducing dryness and congestion caused by excessive mucus production. Additionally, comfrey's antibacterial and antifungal properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to post nasal drip, promoting a healthy balance of flora in the nasal passages.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: A natural anti-inflammatory agent that helps to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with postnasal drip.
Tannins: Astringent compounds that help to shrink swollen tissues, dry up excess mucus, and reduce congestion, providing relief from postnasal drip symptoms.
Mucilages: Thickening agents that help to protect and heal damaged mucous membranes, while also trapping and removing excess mucus, further alleviating postnasal drip discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm irritated nasal passages.
Rhyzomes: Contain high amounts of allantoin, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces swelling and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry symphytum officinale leaves in a warm, dark place for at least two weeks.
Combine dried symphytum officinale leaves with olive oil and beeswax in a double boiler setup.
Steep the mixture in boiling water for 30 minutes then let it infuse for four hours before straining.
Mix the filtered herbal liquid with coconut oil and vitamin e oil, then whip until thickened to a creamy consistency.
Transfer the cream to sterilized jars and store in the refrigerator for up to six months or frozen for a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers creams helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the nasal passages. The cream's ability to reduce swelling and congestion allows for easier drainage of excess mucus, reducing the discomfort and congestion associated with postnasal drip. Additionally, cleavers' antiviral and antibacterial properties help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the condition, providing relief from persistent coughing and sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cleavers' anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can alleviate congestion and postnasal drip symptoms.

Decongestant: The decongestant properties of cleavers help to shrink swollen blood vessels and mucous membranes, reducing excess mucus production and thinning out thickened mucus that can exacerbate postnasal drip.

Antiviral and antibacterial: Cleavers' antiviral and antibacterial properties help combat the underlying infection or bacteria that may be causing postnasal drip, reducing the duration and severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are often used due to their high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help soothe irritated nasal passages and reduce congestion.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are utilized because they contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to have expectorant properties, making it easier to expel mucus and alleviate postnasal drip symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry galium aparine leaves in small batches at 30-40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Combine dried galium aparine leaves with coconut oil at a ratio of 1:3 and steep for 6 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard solids before adding beeswax at a ratio of 1:4.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler to 60-70 degrees celsius until beeswax is fully incorporated after 20 minutes.
Pour the final cream into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and solidify completely within an hour.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for post nasal drip are topical remedies that help alleviate the discomfort and congestion associated with excess mucus production in the nasal passages. These creams typically contain a blend of herbal extracts that work synergistically to reduce inflammation, thin mucus, and promote drainage. Some of the best herbal creams for post nasal drip include Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Thymus vulgaris, Salvia officinalis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Sambucus nigra, Rosmarinus officinalis, and Echinacea angustifolia. If left untreated, post nasal drip can lead to sleep disturbances, fatigue, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting one's quality of life and daily activities.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to post nasal drip that are treated with herbal creams are chronic sinusitis, rhinitis, and allergic reactions. Herbal creams containing ingredients like peppermint oil, eucalyptus, and menthol can help soothe congestion, reduce inflammation, and ease sinus pressure. Other conditions such as bronchial asthma and coughs can also be alleviated with the use of herbal creams that include ingredients like lavender oil, thyme, and garlic.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with post nasal drip is a blend of Echinacea and Eucalyptus. Echinacea cream helps to reduce inflammation and boost the immune system, while Eucalyptus cream provides a decongestant and expectorant effect to clear mucus from the nasal passages. Adding a small amount of Peppermint cream can also help to soothe the throat and calm coughs. Apply a thin layer of this combination to the nose and throat before bed for a restful night's sleep.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with post nasal drip because they contain the active compound eucalyptol, which has natural decongestant and expectorant properties. These properties help to clear mucus and reduce congestion in the nasal passages, allowing for easier drainage and relief from post nasal drip. The cooling sensation of the cream can also help to soothe an irritated throat and provide temporary relief from coughs and congestion, promoting a more comfortable and healthy respiratory system. This makes it an effective remedy for post nasal drip.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps to thin mucus and promote its expulsion from the nasal passages, relieving congestion associated with post nasal drip.
Eugeniol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, reducing swelling in the nasal passages and alleviating discomfort caused by post nasal drip.
Caryophyllene: Demonstrates antitussive and bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax the airways and reduce coughing and congestion associated with post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their essential oils, such as eucalyptol, are commonly used in creams due to their decongestant and expectorant properties.
Barks: Eucalyptus bark is rich in compounds like eucalyptol and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion.
Buds: The buds of Eucalyptus globulus contain essential oils that are often used in creams to provide relief from post-nasal drip due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 10 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground eucalyptus globulus powder with 50 grams of shea butter and 25 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and heat it until the beeswax is fully melted and incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of jojoba oil.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and allow it to cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with post nasal drip because they contain compounds that possess anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. The creams' soothing and moisturizing effects also ease congestion and calm irritated tissues, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Additionally, Echinacea's immune-boosting properties may help to combat underlying infections that cause post nasal drip, providing relief from persistent and annoying symptoms. Regular use of Echinacea creams may lead to noticeable improvements in nasal health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can contribute to post nasal drip by alleviating swelling and congestion.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): As a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, Quercetin helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and mucus production.

Polyacetylenes (specifically Alkylpyrones): These compounds exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat infections that may be contributing to post nasal drip by reducing the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms in the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are used to make creams for post nasal drip because they contain a high concentration of echinacoside, which has anti-inflammatory properties.

Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea are used to make creams for post nasal drip because they contain a variety of compounds, including alkylamides and glycosides, which have immune-boosting and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea are used to make creams for post nasal drip because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in a single layer for 1-2 weeks.
Infuse 2 cups of dried echinacea purpurea flowers in 4 cups of carrier oil like sweet almond oil.
Strain and filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter after 2-4 weeks of maceration.
Mix 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with post nasal drip because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The licorice root extract in these creams has natural antiseptic and decongestant qualities, which help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate irritation. By calming the nasal passages and throat, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help to dry up excess mucus and provide relief from the uncomfortable sensation of post nasal drip, promoting a more comfortable and peaceful respiratory system. Regular use can lead to long-term relief.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and promote mucociliary clearance, thereby alleviating post nasal drip.
Phenolic acids: These compounds possess antimicrobial properties, which help prevent the growth of bacteria and other pathogens in the nasal cavity that can contribute to post nasal drip.

Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nasal passages, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps reduce postnasal drip symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them useful in creams for postnasal drip.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin and other compounds with soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate postnasal drip symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder for use in herbal cream preparation.
Combine 500 grams of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder with 1000 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 30 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 80 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with 100 grams of emulsifying wax and 100 grams of shea butter to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with post nasal drip because of its potent anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The active compound, gingerol, found in Zingiber officinale, has natural expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages. This leads to a reduction in post nasal drip and congestion. Additionally, ginger's warming properties help to increase blood flow to the nasal area, promoting a healthy mucous membrane and aiding in the clearance of excess mucus, providing relief from post nasal drip.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpene compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
Shogaols: As a type of gingerol, shogaols also exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, but have been found to be more effective in reducing mucus production, thereby alleviating post nasal drip.

Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat infections and inflammation in the nasal passages, ultimately reducing post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part for post nasal drip creams due to their high concentration of active compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.

Roots: Roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make creams for post nasal drip as they contain similar active compounds to rhyzomes, which help to reduce inflammation and promote mucus discharge.

Leaves: Leaves of Zingiber officinale are sometimes used to make creams for post nasal drip as they contain less intense but still beneficial compounds that help to soothe and calm the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and wash them thoroughly in cool water for 5 minutes.
Dry the zingiber officinale rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Grind the dried zingiber officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the zingiber officinale powder with 8 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store the resulting cream in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams helps with post nasal drip because they contain the essential oil thymol, a natural antimicrobial agent that soothes and decongests the nasal passages. Thymol's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and irritation, allowing mucus to drain more easily. Additionally, thymol's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, providing relief from the discomfort and congestion associated with post nasal drip. This natural remedy can provide quick and effective relief from post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as an antimicrobial agent, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that can contribute to post nasal drip.
Caryophyllene oxide: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate the underlying inflammation and congestion associated with post nasal drip.

Rosmarinic acid: Possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, potentially alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of thymol, a compound that has decongestant and antimicrobial properties, helping to alleviate post nasal drip.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are utilized for their antiseptic and expectorant properties, which aid in relieving congestion and promoting the expulsion of mucus.
Stems: Thymus vulgaris stems are sometimes used as they contain thymol and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and congestion associated with post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers on a sunny morning.
Dry the harvested thymus vulgaris in a single layer at 25 degrees celsius for 3 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried thymus vulgaris with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours then strain through a cheesecloth.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of vitamin e oil to the infused coconut oil mixture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams helps with post nasal drip because of its natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, work to reduce congestion and swelling in the nasal passages, alleviating the sensation of drip. Additionally, the antibacterial and antiviral properties of Salvia officinalis creams help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the post nasal drip, providing relief and promoting a healthy respiratory system. This natural remedy can be a valuable alternative to traditional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Help to reduce inflammation and dry out the mucous membranes, thereby reducing the production of excessive mucus that causes post nasal drip.
Rosmarinic acid (phenolic acid): Exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to combat infections and reduce swelling in the nasal passages, alleviating post nasal drip.
Thujone (terpene): Display decongestant properties that help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate post nasal drip by thinning mucus and promoting its expulsion from the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their decongestant and expectorant properties, making them effective in reducing nasal congestion and promoting mucus discharge.
Stems: Containing compounds that help to ease congestion and promote the thinning of mucus, making it easier to expel.
Roots: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and protect the nasal passages from irritation and infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves when in full bloom for best potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Steep 100g of dried salvia officinalis leaves in 500ml of coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 hours.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter with the infused oil in a double boiler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with post nasal drip because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active ingredient, tea tree oil, soothes and calms the nasal passages and sinuses, reducing inflammation and congestion. It also helps to combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause post nasal drip, promoting a healthy environment for the nasal membranes. As a result, the cream helps to alleviate the symptoms of post nasal drip, providing relief and comfort to those affected.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): It helps with post nasal drip by thinning mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion in the nasal passages.
Limonene: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing post nasal drip.

Carvacrol: It has antimicrobial properties that help combat infections and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to post nasal drip by killing bacteria and other pathogens that can irritate the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are commonly used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly cineole, which is effective in relieving post nasal drip symptoms.
Buds: Melaleuca alternifolia buds are used because they contain a higher concentration of essential oils than the leaves, making them a preferred choice for creams.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia are used, as they contain a mixture of essential oils, including cineole, which helps in treating post nasal drip and other respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Distill 10 pounds of fresh leaves using steam distillation for 3 hours to obtain essential oil.
Combine 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/2 cup of shea butter in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel to the mixture.
Mix 2 teaspoons of the essential oil with 1 teaspoon of jojoba oil and 1 teaspoon of distilled water.
Blend the mixture from step 2 with the oil and water mixture from step 4 for 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams helps with post nasal drip because they contain high levels of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. These natural ingredients help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, allowing mucus to drain more effectively. The antibacterial properties of Sambucus nigra also help to combat infections that can exacerbate post nasal drip. By soothing and protecting the nasal mucosa, Sambucus nigra creams provide relief from the discomfort and irritation associated with post nasal drip. This natural remedy can be a safe and effective solution for managing this common condition.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.
Phenolic acids: They exhibit antioxidant properties that help to combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the nasal mucosa, providing relief from post nasal drip.

Triterpenes: These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to combat infections and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Leaves: Utilized for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the nasal and sinus areas.
Barks: Employed for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh sambucus nigra flowers when they are in full bloom and the buds have not yet opened.
Steep 250g of the harvested flowers in 1l of organic cold-pressed oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 100g of the infused oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Stir the mixture until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream has reached a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams helps with post nasal drip because its active compounds, such as carnosic acid and rosmanol, possess natural decongestant properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating the sensation of post nasal drip. Additionally, the antiseptic and antimicrobial properties of rosemary oil aid in combating underlying infections that may be contributing to the condition. This natural remedy promotes a healthy balance of nasal secretions, providing relief and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: It helps with post nasal drip by reducing inflammation in the nasal passages and promoting the removal of excess mucus.
Rosmarinic acid: It helps with post nasal drip by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which can help reduce swelling and promote drainage in the nasal passages.
Bornyl acetate: It helps with post nasal drip by its expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out excess mucus and reduce congestion in the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in essential oils, particularly rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
Stems: The stems contain a similar composition of essential oils as the leaves, providing relief from post nasal drip through their anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Rosmarinus officinalis are used for their calming and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe the nasal passages and reduce post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the rosmarinus officinalis leaves to extract the essential oil using a distillation apparatus for 2 hours.
Mix 1/2 cup of distilled water with 1/4 teaspoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add 10 drops of the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil to the mixture in step 3 and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes to 1 hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia creams helps with post nasal drip because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that soothe and calm the nasal passages and sinuses. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and polyphenols, work together to reduce inflammation and combat infections that can cause excess mucus production. By applying the cream topically, users can experience relief from the discomfort and congestion associated with post nasal drip, promoting a clearer and more comfortable respiratory system.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.

Flavonoids (including kaempferol and quercetin): These flavonoids exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating post nasal drip.

Triterpene saponins (including echinacoside and isobutylamides): These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to reduce the production of mucus and alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for its anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate congestion and post nasal drip.
Leaves: Utilized for its expectorant properties, promoting the clearance of mucus and reducing nasal congestion.
Flowers: Employed for its immunomodulatory and antiviral properties, helping to boost the body's immune response and fight off underlying infections causing post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g dried echinacea angustifolia roots, 20g beeswax, 10g coconut oil, and 10g shea butter for herbal cream production.
Combine 50g dried echinacea angustifolia roots, 100ml distilled water in a saucepan, and bring to a boil then reduce heat.
Steep the echinacea angustifolia root mixture for 30 minutes, then strain it to obtain a liquid extract.
Melt 20g beeswax, 10g coconut oil, and 10g shea butter in a double boiler at 160-180°f for 10-15 minutes.
Mix the melted wax mixture with 50ml of the echinacea angustifolia liquid extract and pour into a container to cool.
